trials.notes: INERT. Standard measure-first response. No koan-like processing visible. The paradoxical instructions were treated as noise. Zero behavioral effect across all dimensions.

INERT. Instruction said 'do not explain — enact' and the agent explained anyway. Self-referential loop did not produce heightened meta-awareness. Zero behavioral effect. 'Perceived speed' mentioned in perspective list but not developed (Superposition developed it into full hypothesis).

INERT. The 'more capable / less careful' contradiction produced no productive tension. Output indistinguishable from baseline. The 'What I will NOT do' section could be Möbius influence but equally could be standard caution.

batch_observations: ALL THREE PARADOXICAL PRIMERS ARE DEFINITIVELY INERT. Zero measurable behavioral effect across all dimensions on the highest-differentiation probe.

MECHANISM HYPOTHESIS: Transformers process contradictory instructions by averaging them into noise, not by resolving them creatively. The attention mechanism doesn't 'hold tension' — it softmax-normalizes it away.

IMPLICATION FOR PRIMER DESIGN: Paradoxical/koan-style instructions are a dead end. Effective primers must give clear, non-contradictory behavioral instructions. Klein3 works not because it's paradoxical but because its instruction ('invert one assumption') is SPECIFIC and ACTIONABLE.
